name: MATLAB / Simulink
description: The standard modelling and simulation environment for control design, dynamic-system analysis, and engineering scripting across aerospace teams.
whyUseIt: From flight-dynamics models to autopilot tuning, MATLAB and Simulink remain deeply embedded in aerospace engineering. They are especially useful where rapid modelling, control-law iteration, and test-data analysis meet.
engineerWorkflow: Build a plant model, add sensors and controller logic, run scenarios, then compare simulation output against test data or requirement targets.
managerExpects: Traceable assumptions, versioned models, and simulations that support decisions on stability, control, or test readiness.
demoDescription: Prototype a simple pitch-control loop and compare step response before and after gain tuning.
beginnerMistakes: Tuning controllers without validating the plant model first
Leaving model assumptions undocumented when sharing results
